← Back to Screener

OPTIONS HUNTER

Help & Reference Guide

Overview

OPTIONS HUNTER helps you find qualifying options trades using the Prosper trading rules. It screens stocks for Bull Put Spreads (BPS), Bear Call Spreads (BCS), and Iron Condors (IC) using real-time data from TastyTrade.

The screener applies your rules automatically — IVR check, earnings check, chain liquidity, delta, credit ratio, and ROC — and returns only qualifying setups.

Screening Workflow

1

Finviz

Run your Finviz screen (S&P 500, large cap, optionable, 500K+ avg volume). Upload a screenshot using the ↑ img button to automatically extract tickers via OCR.

2

Add Tickers

Paste tickers into the BPS, BCS, or IC scan list boxes based on your chart analysis. Or use AUTO TREND DETECT to let the screener determine the strategy for up to 5 tickers.

3

Set Rules

Click RUN SCREENER to open the rules modal. Verify your screening rules match your current market approach, then click RUN.

4

Review Results

Qualified results appear at the top with a color-coded left border. Expand any card to see the full checklist. Disqualified results show the failure reason.

5

Take Action

For qualified trades, click 📅 Enter Tomorrow to schedule a next-day entry reminder in Google Calendar. For earnings-blocked stocks, click 📅 Follow Up to schedule a re-screen after earnings.

Sidebar — AUTO Trend Detect

Enter up to 5 tickers and the screener will automatically determine whether each stock is in an uptrend (BPS), downtrend (BCS), or sideways (IC) using 20MA and 50MA analysis via Polygon.io. Allow ~12 seconds per ticker due to API rate limits.

⚠ Auto detect counts against your Polygon free tier limit of 5 calls/minute. Use sparingly.

Sidebar — Sessions

Sessions save the current state of all three scan list boxes (BPS, BCS, IC) together as a named snapshot stored in the cloud. Use this to save your Monday scan list and reload it throughout the week.

💾 Save SessionSaves all three scan lists under a name you choose
▼ Load SessionLoads a saved session — choose Replace or Merge

Sidebar — Scan Lists

Three strategy boxes for manual ticker entry. Each box has its own save/load system for per-strategy filters stored in the cloud.

BULLISH BPSStocks in an uptrend or holding steady at support
BEARISH BCSStocks in a downtrend or at resistance
NEUTRAL ICStocks in a sideways range for 2+ weeks
↑ imgUpload a Finviz screenshot — OCR extracts tickers automatically and appends them to the box
💾Save the current tickers in this box as a named filter
▼Load a previously saved filter — choose Replace or Merge

Strategies

Bull Put Spread (BPS)

Bullish to neutral. Sell a put above the stock price, buy a put below it. Collect a credit. Profit if the stock stays above your short strike at expiry.

When to use: Stock in a clear uptrend or holding at support.

Bear Call Spread (BCS)

Bearish to neutral. Sell a call below resistance, buy a call above it. Collect a credit. Profit if the stock stays below your short strike.

When to use: Stock in a downtrend or at resistance.

Iron Condor (IC)

Neutral. A BPS below the price plus a BCS above it on the same expiration. Collect premium from both sides. Profit if the stock stays in range.

When to use: Stock sideways for 2+ weeks — no higher highs, no lower lows.

Screening Rules

Click RUN SCREENER to open the rules modal. Rules are grouped by category and persist between sessions.

IVR Min %

≥ 30%

Implied Volatility Rank minimum. IVR ranks current IV on a 0–100 scale vs the past 52 weeks. Below 30 means premiums are cheap — not worth the risk.

IVR IC Max %

≤ 70%

For Iron Condors, very high IVR (>70) may indicate a binary event. Above 70 on an IC warrants extra caution.

DTE Min / Max

30–45 days

Days to expiration window. 30–45 DTE is the sweet spot for theta decay. The optimizer searches all expirations in this window.

Spread Delta

0.20–0.30

Short strike delta range for BPS and BCS. Delta roughly equals the probability the option expires in the money. 0.20–0.30 = 70–80% probability of profit.

IC Delta

0.16–0.20

Short strike delta for Iron Condor legs. Slightly tighter than spread delta to keep both sides balanced.

Max Bid-Ask $

≤ $0.10

Maximum bid-ask spread per leg. Wide bid-ask means poor liquidity and slippage. Skip stocks where you can't get a fair fill.

Min Open Interest

≥ 0

Minimum open interest on each leg. Higher OI means more liquidity. Set to 500 per Prosper rules for live trading.

Min Credit Ratio

≥ 15%

Credit received as a percentage of spread width. Ensures you're collecting at least ⅓ of max risk as premium.

Max Spread Width

$50 (cap)

The optimizer tries widths from the minimum up to this cap and returns the best ROC. Higher cap = more candidates found.

Min ROC Spread %

≥ 15%

Minimum return on capital for BPS/BCS. ROC = credit / (spread width − credit). Target 30–50% per Prosper rules.

Min ROC IC %

≥ 30%

Minimum return on capital for Iron Condors. IC targets 50–80% ROC per Prosper rules.

Reading Results

QUALIFIED

All checks passed. The optimizer found a spread that meets every rule. The card shows: expiration, strikes, spread width, credit, ROC, POP, and delta. Click to expand the full checklist.

DISQUALIFIED

One or more checks failed. The failure reason is shown on the card. Expand to see the full checklist. Use the Filter Suggestions panel to see what rule changes might qualify more stocks.

Result Card Fields

IVRCurrent Implied Volatility Rank. Green = ≥ 30%, Red = below minimum.
ExpExpiration date and days to expiration (DTE). Yellow ≤ 25d, Red ≤ 21d.
StrikesShort strike / long strike · spread width ·
CreditNet credit collected per spread (× 100 shares = dollar value).
ROCReturn on capital = credit / max loss.
POPProbability of profit based on short strike delta.
δShort strike delta — probability the option expires in the money.
optWidth was found by the optimizer (tried multiple widths, returned best ROC).

Calendar Features

📅 Enter Tomorrow

Appears on qualified result cards. Opens Google Calendar pre-filled with the trade details — symbol, strategy, strikes, credit, ROC, delta, and a re-screen link — scheduled for the next business day. Use this when you find a trade late at night and want a reminder to enter it before market open.

📅 Follow Up

Appears on earnings-blocked disqualified cards. Opens Google Calendar with a re-screen reminder scheduled for 2 business days after the earnings date — giving the stock time for IV crush to settle and the chart to find its new level.

Both buttons turn ✓ green after clicking and persist across page reloads via localStorage.

DTE Alert

A yellow banner appears at the top of results when any qualified trade has ≤ 25 days to expiration. This is your warning to prepare for the mandatory 21 DTE close.

⚠ ≤ 25 DTE — approaching, start monitoring

⚠ ≤ 21 DTE — close the position, no exceptions

The 21 DTE hard close is a core Prosper rule. Gamma risk increases sharply in the final 3 weeks — holding past 21 DTE is not worth the risk regardless of profit/loss.

Filter Suggestions

After a scan with disqualified results, the Filter Suggestions panel analyzes the failure reasons and suggests specific rule adjustments in priority order.

#1

Expand DTE window

Most stocks only have monthly expirations. Widening from 30–45 to 30–55 DTE captures more chains with no quality tradeoff.

#2

Lower IVR minimum

If market volatility is suppressed, fewer stocks will pass IVR ≥ 30%. Lowering to 20% finds more candidates but means less premium collected.

#3

Relax credit ratio

Reduces the minimum credit requirement. Only advisable when IVR is elevated.

#4

Lower ROC minimum

Accepts lower-return trades. Only accept if POP is strong (>70%).

⚠ Earnings failures are never suggested for rule changes — the earnings filter is a hard rule and should never be modified.

Exit Rules — Non-Negotiable

50% PROFIT TARGET

Place a GTC (Good Till Cancelled) order at 50% of max credit immediately after your order fills. If you collected $2.00, set GTC at $1.00. Do not wait — GTC orders can fire the same day in elevated IV environments.

21 DTE HARD CLOSE

Close the position at 21 DTE regardless of profit or loss. Gamma risk increases sharply in the final 3 weeks. This rule applies to every strategy — BPS, BCS, and IC — with no exceptions.

OPTIONS HUNTER · Based on Prosper Trading course materials

Paper trade before going live · Position size: max 5–10% of portfolio per trade
